question: In a simple Java class with no inheritance, when is the constructor executed?
options: When an object is created using the 'new' keyword
When the class is loaded by the JVM
When a static method of the class is called
When the program starts
explanation: A constructor is called to initialize a new object, which happens at the moment the object is instantiated with the 'new' keyword.

question: In a class hierarchy (Parent and Child classes), what is the order of constructor execution when creating a Child object?
options: Parent constructor first, then Child constructor
Child constructor first, then Parent constructor
Only the Child constructor is executed
The order is random and determined by the JVM
explanation: Java ensures that a parent class is fully initialized before its child class. This is achieved by an implicit call to super() as the first statement in the child constructor, leading to the parent constructor executing first.

options: ABC
CBA
C
A
explanation: Due to constructor chaining, the execution begins at the top of the inheritance hierarchy. The constructor for A runs first (printing "A"), then B (printing "B"), and finally C (printing "C"), resulting in the output "ABC".

options: The constructor Derived(int num) will cause a compilation error
The constructor Derived() will correctly call the parent constructor
Both constructors will compile and run without error
The class Base does not need an explicit constructor
explanation: The class Base has a parameterized constructor, so the default no-arg constructor is not provided by the compiler. The constructor Derived(int num) does not have an explicit call to super(...) as its first line, so the compiler will try to insert super() (a call to a non-existent no-arg constructor in Base), resulting in a compilation error.

options: SIC
SCI
CIS
ISC
explanation: First, the class is loaded, executing the static block (printing 'S'). Then, when the object is instantiated with 'new', the instance initialization block runs (printing 'I') before the body of the constructor itself executes (printing 'C'). The order is always: 1. Static init, 2. Instance init, 3. Constructor.
